mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and margin pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >58% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 5.0%, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined condition yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >62% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual deterioration condition is followed by positive 5-day returns in >57% of cases, or the signal fails to generate significant alpha after transaction cost adjustments.
